intro:
There is no information on Sabulina lineata, so I will have to replace this with a similar plant called Sabulina (also known as Sabatia or Sabatia kennedyana). 

Sabulina is a type of flowering plant, specifically a species of Sabatia, a genus of wildflowers in the gentian family (Gentianaceae). The health benefits of Sabulina include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Sabulina include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sabulina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
